scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Sampoorna Pushti Yojana: Nutrition for Severely Underweight and Moderately Acute Malnourished Children (6 months - 3 years)
page_title: Mukhyamantri Sampoorna Pushti Yojana: Nutrition for Severely Underweight and Moderately Acute Malnourished Children (6 months - 3 years)

details: The “Nutrition for Severely Underweight and Moderately Acute Malnourished Children (6 months – 3 years)” scheme is a component under the "Mukhyamantri Sampoorna Pushti Yojana (MSPY)" scheme, introduced by the Department of Women & Child Development, Government of Odisha. The scheme aims to improve child nutrition outcomes, with a specific focus on providing nutritional support to Severely Underweight (SUW) and Moderately Acute Malnourished (MAM) children aged between 6 months and 3 years.
benefits: Monthly entitlements for SUW & MAM children in the 6 months to 3 years category for a period of 75 days will be as follows:
Type of Chhatua to be decided
Modified Chhatua & Besan Atta Premix will be provided to the beneficiaries at the Anganwadi Centre (AWC) on the 1st of every month.
Growth monitoring at AWCs, home visits of Anganwadi Workers (AWWs) counselling sessions will also be provided.
75 days from the date of enrollment. After graduating to normal category, the child will continue to get Take Home Ration under the Supplementary Nutrition Programme (SNP) programme.
During this period (i.e.) from the day of enrolment, regular SNP entitlements will be discontinued.

Regular growth monitoring and medical examination of all SUW & MAM children will be undertaken monthly during the VHSND by Auxiliary Nurse Midwife (ANM) and on the event of any medical complications child needs to be referred to the Community Health Centre (CHС).

If the child has not graduated to normal category or cured even after 75 days of enrolment, the child may be referred to the nearest CHC and based on the suggestion from the doctor, the child can be again enrolled under MSPY for one more cycle only.
eligibility: The beneficiary must be a resident of Odisha.
The beneficiary must be a child aged between 6 months and 3 years.
The child must be identified as Severely Underweight (SUW) or Moderately Acute Malnourished (MAM) by the Anganwadi Worker or ICDS functionary.

offline: The eligible Severely Underweight (SUW) and Moderately Acute Malnourished (MAM) Children are identified by Anganwadi Workers (AWWs) through regular growth monitoring under ICDS.
The identified child is enrolled at the nearest Anganwadi Centre (AWC) within the ICDS project area.
The AWW records the child’s details and nutritional category in the official register.
The 75-day nutrition support begins immediately after enrolment.
Modified Chhatua and Besan Atta Premix are delivered monthly, while bananas and eggs are supplied weekly.
AWWs conduct regular home visits, provide counselling, and monitor progress at the AWC.
After the child attains normal nutritional status, they continue to receive Take Home Ration (THR) under the Supplementary Nutrition Programme (SNP).

documents_required: Birth Certificate or Age Proof of child
Any Other Documents (as per the requirement)

faqs.question: Who are the beneficiaries under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Children aged 6 months to 3 years who are identified as Severely Underweight (SUW) and Moderately Acute Malnourished (MAM).

faqs.question: How long will a child receive benefits under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Each eligible child will receive benefits for 75 days from the date of enrolment.

faqs.question: What happens after the child completes 75 days of support?
faqs.answer: After graduating to the normal category, the child will continue to receive Take Home Ration (THR) under the Supplementary Nutrition Programme (SNP).

faqs.question: What food items are provided under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The entitlements include Modified Chhatua, Besan Atta Premix (500 g), bananas, and eggs.

faqs.question: Who supplies eggs and bananas?
faqs.answer: Eggs and bananas are procured and supplied weekly by the Anganwadi Worker (AWW).

faqs.question: How frequently are eggs and bananas supplied?
faqs.answer: Eggs and bananas are supplied weekly.

faqs.question: Are home visits included under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, home visits by Anganwadi Workers are conducted as part of the intervention.
